project.name: Euler OMR
project.description: A production-grade desktop application for designing OMR (Optical Mark Recognition) templates, scanning and marking student answer sheets, and running statistical analysis on grading results.

layout.main.py: Application entry point; bootstraps QApplication, splash screen, and MainWindow

euler_omr/.app.py: QApplication subclass; global exception handler; single-instance guard
euler_omr/.constants.py: All magic numbers, file extensions, limits, and version string
euler_omr/.config.py: Persistent QSettings wrapper (geometry, recents, theme preferences)
euler_omr/.logger.py: Centralized structlog/logging setup; rotating file handler + Qt signal handler for UI log panel

core/.template_compiler.py: LaTeX template builder; pdflatex/TinyTeX detection and invocation; progress callbacks
core/.scan_reader.py: PDF-to-image conversion (pypdfium2); corner-mark detection; OMR bubble reader; auto-contrast adjuster
core/.grader.py: Answer-key application; score computation per student per version
core/.analysis.py: Statistical engine (mean, median, mode, stddev); per-version breakdown; question-level diagnostics; fairness verdict
core/.report_builder.py: LaTeX analysis report composer; chart generation (matplotlib -> PDF embed)

models/.template_model.py: TemplateConfig dataclass: institution_name, logo_path, id_digits, num_versions, num_questions, num_options; serialization helpers
models/.project_model.py: ProjectConfig dataclass: template_ref, scans_ref, active_questions, active_options, active_versions, answer_keys, scan_results; serialization helpers
models/.scan_result.py: ScanResult dataclass per page: page_no, student_id, version, answers[], state(SUCCESS|NEEDS_REVIEW|RESOLVED); issue list

file_io/.eomrt_handler.py: Read/write *.eomrt: base64-packed JSON bundle (config + compiled PDF bytes + logo bytes)
file_io/.eomrp_handler.py: Read/write *.eomrp: base64-packed JSON bundle (project config + embedded template + scans reference + scan results + answer keys)

workers/.base_worker.py: QRunnable base with QObject signals mixin: progress(int), log(str, level), finished(), error(str)
workers/.compile_worker.py: Runs template compilation in QThreadPool; emits line-by-line log from pdflatex stdout
workers/.scan_worker.py: Reads all PDF pages sequentially; emits per-page ScanResult and progress percentage
workers/.grade_worker.py: Applies answer keys and runs analysis; emits progress and final report path

ui/.main_window.py: MainWindow(QMainWindow): central QTabWidget (TDI); menu bar; status bar; recent-files management; drag-and-drop *.eomrt / *.eomrp onto tab bar
ui/.style.py: Full QSS stylesheet string built from the brand color palette; exposes apply_stylesheet(app)
ui/.fonts.py: Fixedsys font loader (bundled TTF fallback if system font absent)

widgets/.log_panel.py: QPlainTextEdit subclass styled as a terminal; color-coded by log level; auto-scroll; copy-to-clipboard action
widgets/.progress_overlay.py: Semi-transparent overlay widget with QProgressBar and cancel button for long operations
widgets/.image_preview.py: Zoomable/pannable QGraphicsView for template PDF preview
widgets/.bubble_crop_view.py: Read-only QLabel with QPixmap crop display for Review Page dialogue

tabs/.welcome_tab.py: WelcomeTab(QWidget): left panel (linked labels for create/open actions, recent files list); right panel (What is New label); bottom panel (GitHub link label)
tabs/.template_tab.py: TemplateTab(QWidget): left form panel; right PDF preview panel; bottom log panel; dirty-state tracking; close-guard
tabs/.project_tab.py: ProjectTab(QWidget): left controls panel; right scan results table; bottom log panel; dirty-state tracking; close-guard

dialogs/.answer_key_dialog.py: AnswerKeyDialog(QDialog): version tabs; question rows with option checkboxes; save/ignore buttons; validates no empty question
dialogs/.review_page_dialog.py: ReviewPageDialog(QDialog): left nav panel with color-coded issue states; right panel with bubble crops and correction widgets (QComboBox/QLineEdit); save/ignore buttons
dialogs/.tinytex_dialog.py: TinyTexInstallDialog(QDialog): explanation label; download & install button; progress bar; log output
dialogs/.unsaved_changes_dialog.py: Reusable QDialog: Save / Discard / Cancel buttons

assets/.icons/: SVG/PNG icons for toolbar and tab close buttons (no emojis)

contrast_rules: All body text on dark backgrounds must use text_on_dark (#f0f6f6) — never text_secondary
Disabled controls use text_disabled (#8caaa4) on bg_surface — minimum 3:1 contrast ratio
Error/Warning/Info/Success labels must always appear on bg_surface or bg_deep, never on primary_normal
Log panel: INFO = text_on_dark, WARNING = warning_normal, ERROR = error_normal, DEBUG = text_secondary
Issue rows in scan table: NEEDS_REVIEW row background tint = warning_normal at 15% alpha; RESOLVED = success_normal at 15% alpha; SUCCESS = transparent
qss_notes: Apply a single global QSS stylesheet via QApplication.setStyleSheet() at startup from ui/style.py
Use object names (setObjectName) for targeted QSS selectors to avoid cascade bleed
Tab bar close buttons use custom SVG icon, not the system X glyph
QTabWidget tabBar uses bg_surface background, primary_normal bottom-border for active tab, border_dark for inactive
All QScrollBar handles: primary_normal; track: bg_deep
QPushButton normal: bg = secondary_bold, text = text_on_dark; hover: primary_normal; pressed: primary_pressed; disabled: bg_muted text_disabled
QLineEdit / QSpinBox / QComboBox border: border_dark; focus border: primary_normal; background: bg_surface; text: text_on_dark

items.description: QListWidget showing recent *.eomrp and *.eomrt files; double-click opens the file; right-click context menu: Open, Remove from Recents

items.text: What is New in Euler OMR v1.0.0

items.content: Initial release. Template designer with LaTeX compilation. OMR scan reader with auto-contrast. Statistical analysis report generator. Full project save/load.

template_tab.close_guard: If template has unsaved changes, show UnsavedChangesDialog before closing
template_tab.dirty_indicator: Append * to tab title when unsaved changes exist

buttons.label: Compile and Save PDF
buttons.description: Checks for pdflatex/TinyTeX; if absent shows TinyTexInstallDialog; else runs CompileWorker in QThreadPool; on success opens SaveFileDialog for *.pdf; enables btn_save_template

buttons.label: Save Template
buttons.description: Saves *.eomrt via EomrTHandler; if file path already known, overwrites silently; else opens SaveFileDialog

right_panel.description: Zoomable/pannable PDF preview rendered from compiled PDF via pypdfium2; placeholder message shown before first compile

bottom_panel.description: Streams pdflatex stdout/stderr line by line; color-coded log levels; auto-scroll; clear button

project_tab.close_guard: If project has unsaved changes, show UnsavedChangesDialog before closing
project_tab.dirty_indicator: Append * to tab title when unsaved changes exist

controls.label: Active Questions
controls.note: Max is capped to loaded template's num_questions; disabled until template loaded

controls.label: Start Reading Scans
controls.re_run_warning: Warn that resolved issue decisions will be cleared; user must confirm before re-run

controls.label: Save Project (*.eomrp)
controls.description: Always available after first data entry; overwrites if path known

controls.label: Run Grading
controls.description: Opens SaveFileDialog for *.xlsx output; optionally triggers PDF analysis report if chk_run_analysis is checked

scan_results_table.row_coloring: NEEDS_REVIEW = warning tint, RESOLVED = success tint, SUCCESS = no tint

summary_labels.style: error_normal when n > 0, success_normal when n == 0

bottom_panel.description: Per-page scan log; grading log; structured entries with timestamps

answer_key_dialog.title: Manage Answer Keys
answer_key_dialog.description: Tabbed by version (A, B, C, ... up to active_versions). Each tab shows a scrollable list of Q1..Q{active_questions}, each with QCheckBox per active option (A..{active_option_letter}). At least one option must be selected per question — show inline error_normal label if none selected on save. Pre-populates with previously saved answer key if available.

id_and_written_data.id_textbox: QLineEdit editable; digits only; length == id_digits; * shown for unread digits

version.version_combobox: QComboBox with active version letters (A, B, C, ...)

question_section.answer_combobox: QComboBox with active option letters plus BLANK

review_page_dialog.state_note: A page state remains NEEDS_REVIEW until ALL its issues are resolved. Each correction persists independently when user clicks Save Changes — user may re-open the dialog to change any decision.

tinytex_install_dialog.title: LaTeX Compiler Not Found
tinytex_install_dialog.description: pdflatex was not found on this system. Euler OMR requires a LaTeX compiler to generate templates. You may install TinyTeX, a minimal distribution (~200 MB).

widgets.text: pdflatex not found. Install TinyTeX to enable template compilation.

unsaved_changes_dialog.title: Unsaved Changes
unsaved_changes_dialog.message_pattern: '{name}' has unsaved changes. Do you want to save before closing?

responsibilities: Locate pdflatex binary via shutil.which; also check TinyTeX default install paths on Windows/macOS/Linux
Accept TemplateConfig and produce a filled LaTeX source string by substituting \newcommand values in the bundled maximum template
Write .tex to a QTemporaryDir and invoke pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode -halt-on-error
Stream stdout/stderr via subprocess.Popen line-by-line; emit log signals
On success, return path to compiled PDF and read bytes for embedding in *.eomrt
Raise TemplateCompileError with last pdflatex error line on failure
Load *.pdf with pypdfium2; render each page to numpy array at 200 DPI
Corner mark detection: use cv2 template matching or contour analysis to find the four rectangular corner marks; identify the wider top-left mark for orientation correction
Orientation correction: if top-left mark is not in the expected corner, rotate image 90/180/270 degrees accordingly
Perspective correction: use the four corner mark centroids as homography source points to dewarp the scanned page to the canonical template coordinate space
Timing mark grid extraction: use left/right timing marks to establish precise row positions for all bubble rows
ID bubble reading: for each of the id_digits columns and 0-9 rows, threshold the bubble region and compute fill ratio; filled bubble = digit value; handle multi-fill and no-fill as issues
Version bubble reading: for each active version bubble, compute fill ratio; handle multi-fill and no-fill as issues
Question bubble reading: for each of active_questions x active_options bubbles, compute fill ratio; handle multi-fill and no-fill as issues

Auto-contrast fallback: if total issue count for a page exceeds a threshold (e.g. 5), apply cv2.equalizeHist or CLAHE on the grayscale page and re-attempt full reading; pick the parameter set yielding fewer issues
Assemble ScanResult per page; emit via signal
Log all per-page outcomes at DEBUG level; log issues at WARNING level
Accept list of ScanResult and AnswerKey
For each ScanResult with state SUCCESS or RESOLVED and no remaining issues: compute score as count of questions where student answer is in the correct answers set for that version
Return list of GradeRecord: student_id, version, score, max_score, answers[]
Skip NEEDS_REVIEW pages and emit a warning log
Accept list of GradeRecord and AnswerKey and active_questions
Overall statistics: mean, median, mode, stddev of all scores
Per-version statistics: mean, median, mode, stddev of scores per version

Per-question per-version: frequency distribution of each chosen option; compute difficulty index (p-value = fraction who answered correctly); classify as EASY (p > 0.75), MODERATE (0.40 <= p <= 0.75), HARD (p < 0.40)
Confusion flag: if no single option exceeds 35% frequency for a question, flag as CONFUSING
Version fairness: compare per-version mean scores; if max_mean - min_mean > 10% of max_score, flag versions as UNFAIR; otherwise FAIR
Return AnalysisReport dataclass containing all the above
Accept AnalysisReport
Generate matplotlib charts: overall grade distribution histogram, per-version boxplot, per-question difficulty bar chart, version comparison bar chart
Save charts as PNG to temp directory
Build a LaTeX report source file embedding the charts via \includegraphics
Compile with pdflatex; return path to analysis PDF
Clean up temp files after compilation
Accept list of GradeRecord
Create workbook with sheet 'Grades': columns [Student ID, Version, Score, Max Score, Percentage]
Apply header styling: primary_normal fill, white bold text
Alternate row shading: bg_surface and bg_muted equivalent in RGB
Auto-fit column widths
If analysis was run, add sheet 'Analysis Summary' with overall and per-version stats table
Save to user-specified path

schema.compiled_pdf_b64: base64-encoded bytes of the compiled PDF, null if not yet compiled
schema.logo_b64: base64-encoded bytes of the logo image file, null if default
schema.logo_filename: original filename with extension for format detection on load

schema.template: embedded eomrt dict (same schema as above) so project is self-contained
schema.scans_pdf_b64: base64-encoded bytes of the scans PDF

workers.name: CompileWorker
workers.ui_response: Disable form inputs and compile button during run; show progress overlay; re-enable on finished or error

workers.name: ScanWorker
workers.ui_response: Update table incrementally via page_result signal; update progress bar; log each page

workers.name: GradeWorker
workers.ui_response: Show progress overlay; open output folder on success

rules: Never access QWidget from a worker thread — all UI updates via signals/slots
Workers must honor a cancel_requested flag; check it between pages/steps
Use QMutex if any shared state is written from a worker

handlers.name: QtLogSignalHandler
handlers.description: Custom logging.Handler subclass that emits a Qt signal per record; connected to LogPanelWidget.append_log slot

levels.DEBUG: Bubble-level fill ratios, timing mark positions
levels.INFO: Page read success, file save success, compilation step start/end
levels.WARNING: Issues detected on a page, skipped NEEDS_REVIEW page in grading
levels.ERROR: pdflatex failure, scan read failure, file I/O error

logging.usage_note: Use structlog.get_logger(__name__) in every module; bind context (page_no, template_name) with logger.bind()

displayed_in: Welcome tab What is New header

recents_management.storage: QSettings key 'recents/files', list of absolute path strings
recents_management.on_open: Prepend to list, remove duplicates, trim to max_entries
recents_management.on_missing_file: Show warning and remove from recents list
recents_management.displayed_in: WelcomeTab recents QListWidget with icon indicating file type (eomrp vs eomrt)

latex_template.strategy: The bundled LaTeX template is the MAXIMUM configuration (14 ID digits, 26 versions, 99 questions, 8 options). The compiler substitutes only the \newcommand values at the top of the file. The template's internal logic already handles rendering only up to the specified counts — no structural LaTeX changes are needed for different configurations.
latex_template.assets_dir: assets/ folder must be placed relative to the temp .tex file during compilation so \includegraphics{assets/logo} resolves correctly; copy logo to assets/logo.{ext} in temp dir before pdflatex run

corner_marks.top_left: Width 2.0cm, height 0.8cm — the WIDER mark; used for orientation detection
corner_marks.detection_hint: After finding 4 rectangles, the widest one is always top-left; use this to determine if page is rotated

timing_marks.left_side: x = 1.2cm from left edge; y starts at 2cm + RowStep from bottom; one mark every RowStep (0.5cm); width 0.4cm, height 0.2cm
timing_marks.right_side: mirror of left side; x = page_width - 1.6cm
timing_marks.first_mark_skipped: y=0 timing mark is intentionally absent to avoid merging with corner marks
timing_marks.usage: Use timing mark y-positions to establish the precise row coordinate grid for bubble reading

bubble_geometry.fill_threshold: Compute mean pixel value inside bubble circle region after binarization; if mean < threshold (e.g. 128 on 0-255 scale) consider bubble filled

id_issues.missing_digit: No bubble filled in a digit column — display as * in ID textbox; user must type the correct digit
id_issues.multi_digit: More than one bubble filled in a digit column — highlight column; user must pick or type correct digit

version_issues.missing_version: No version bubble filled — combobox shows blank; user must select a version
version_issues.multi_version: More than one version bubble filled — combobox shows blank; user must select a version
version_issues.unresolved_leaves_issue: Leaving the combobox at blank does NOT resolve the issue

question_issues.missing_answer: No option bubble filled — combobox shows blank; user may select an option or BLANK (intentionally unanswered)
question_issues.multi_answer: More than one option filled — combobox shows blank; user may select one option or BLANK
question_issues.blank_is_valid: Selecting BLANK is a valid resolution — it means the student left the question blank intentionally

issue_types.page_state_rule: A page transitions from NEEDS_REVIEW to RESOLVED only when ALL its issues have been addressed (no remaining blank comboboxes or unresolved ID digits). SUCCESS pages have zero issues from initial reading and are never demoted.

analysis_report_sections.content: Mean, Median, Mode, Standard Deviation for all students combined; histogram chart of grade distribution
For each active version: Mean, Median, Mode, Standard Deviation; boxplot chart comparing all versions side by side
For each question in each version: frequency table of chosen options; difficulty classification (EASY / MODERATE / HARD); CONFUSING flag if no dominant option; bar chart of option frequencies
Side-by-side comparison of per-version mean scores; bar chart; verdict: VERSIONS ARE FAIR / VERSIONS ARE UNFAIR with explanation; versions ranked from hardest to easiest

implementation_instructions: Do NOT use emojis anywhere in the UI — use text labels and SVG icons only
Ensure WCAG AA minimum contrast between all text colors and their backgrounds as specified in design_system.contrast_rules
Use QThreadPool and QRunnable for all I/O-heavy or CPU-heavy operations — never block the main Qt event loop
Use proper Python logging with structlog; every module obtains its own logger via structlog.get_logger(__name__)
Show QProgressBar widgets for all operations that process multiple items (scans, compilation steps, download)
Apply a single global QSS stylesheet at application startup using the brand color palette defined in design_system.color_palette
Use QSettings to persist window geometry, splitter sizes, and recent files across sessions
Implement dirty-state tracking on both TemplateTab and ProjectTab; set dirty=True on any user edit; clear on successful save; show * in tab title when dirty
All file save operations: if a file path is already known (previously saved), overwrite silently; if new, open QFileDialog.getSaveFileName
Use dataclasses for all data models (TemplateConfig, ProjectConfig, ScanResult, GradeRecord, AnalysisReport) with to_dict() and from_dict() class methods for JSON serialization
The *.eomrt and *.eomrp files are JSON-serialized then base64-encoded; include a 'format_version' key to enable future migration
Embed the full LaTeX maximum template as a string constant in core/template_compiler.py; do not read it from disk at runtime
For the bundled Fixedsys font: load via QFontDatabase.addApplicationFont() at startup from assets/fonts/; fall back to 'Courier New' if not found
The application minimum window size is 960x540; enforce with setMinimumSize(); default opening size is 1280x720
Write unit tests for core modules (template_compiler, scan_reader, grader, analysis) using pytest and pytest-qt
Use pyproject.toml for packaging with a [project.scripts] entry point pointing to main.py:main
Apply semantic versioning; __version__ in euler_omr/__init__.py is the single source of truth
